attributes.description: AWS ECS dashboard for CloudWatch metrics collected by the OpenTelemetry Collector.

embeddableConfig.description:
Monitor your AWS ECS / ECS services in real time.
Pick services via the top-bar selector, or click any row in the ECS services table to filter the dashboard by service / cluster / region.
State badges follow the same palette as the Services-by-state donut (red=saturated, amber=hot, green=healthy, grey=idle).

attributes.description: Navigation tile — clicking opens the Kibana Dashboards library searched for “AWS OTel” dashboards in a new browser tab, carrying the current panel time range to the destination.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
ROW value = "AWS-Managed-OTel" | KEEP value

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
ROW value = "AWS-Managed-OTel" | KEEP value

embeddableConfig.description: Navigation tile — clicking opens the Kibana Dashboards library searched for “AWS OTel” dashboards in a new browser tab, carrying the current panel time range to the destination.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-*
| WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != ""
  AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != ""
| STATS cpu_cur = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))),
    mem_cur = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL))),
    last_seen = MAX(@timestamp)
  BY ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen)
| WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5
| EVAL state = CASE(
    cpu_cur IS NULL AND mem_cur IS NULL, "Unknown",
    cpu_cur > 80 OR mem_cur > 80, "saturated",
    cpu_cur > 50 OR mem_cur > 50, "hot",
    "healthy")
| STATS services = COUNT(*) BY state

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-*
| WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != ""
  AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != ""
| STATS cpu_cur = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))),
    mem_cur = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL))),
    last_seen = MAX(@timestamp)
  BY ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region
| INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen)
| WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5
| EVAL state = CASE(
    cpu_cur IS NULL AND mem_cur IS NULL, "Unknown",
    cpu_cur > 80 OR mem_cur > 80, "saturated",
    cpu_cur > 50 OR mem_cur > 50, "hot",
    "healthy")
| STATS services = COUNT(*) BY state

embeddableConfig.description: Share of ECS services by latest state — saturated (CPU or memory > 80%), hot (> 50%), idle (CPU < 5%), or healthy. Uses the most recent value per service within a 5-minute freshness window. A growing saturated slice indicates services under resource pressure.
embeddableConfig.title: Services by state

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-*
| WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != ""
  AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != ""
| STATS cpu_avg = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))),
    mem_avg = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL)))
  BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region
| EVAL state = CASE(
    cpu_avg IS NULL AND mem_avg IS NULL, "Unknown",
    cpu_avg > 80 OR mem_avg > 80, "saturated",
    cpu_avg > 50 OR mem_avg > 50, "hot",
    "healthy")
| STATS total = COUNT(*),
    saturated = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "saturated",
    hot = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "hot",
    healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "healthy"
  BY timestamp
| SORT timestamp ASC

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-*
| WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != ""
  AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != ""
| STATS cpu_avg = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))),
    mem_avg = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL)))
  BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region
| EVAL state = CASE(
    cpu_avg IS NULL AND mem_avg IS NULL, "Unknown",
    cpu_avg > 80 OR mem_avg > 80, "saturated",
    cpu_avg > 50 OR mem_avg > 50, "hot",
    "healthy")
| STATS total = COUNT(*),
    saturated = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "saturated",
    hot = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "hot",
    healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "healthy"
  BY timestamp
| SORT timestamp ASC

embeddableConfig.description: Count of ECS services over time, classified per time bucket as saturated, hot, healthy, or idle by peak CPU/memory utilization. Distinct services are counted per bucket. A rising saturated series indicates spreading resource pressure.
embeddableConfig.title: Service count over time (by state)

embeddableConfig.description: Number of saturated ECS services — services whose latest CPU or memory utilization exceeds 80%, within a 5-minute freshness window. A non-zero count indicates services under heavy resource pressure that may throttle or be killed.

embeddableConfig.description: Number of hot ECS services — services whose latest CPU or memory utilization is above 50% but not yet saturated (≤ 80%), within a 5-minute freshness window. An early warning of rising resource usage.

One hexagon per ECS service, ordered worst-first with CPU- or memory-saturated services first. Toggle Color by between Status, CPU utilization, or Memory utilization; metric views support Min, Max, Avg, and Last over the dashboard time range. Click any hexagon to filter the dashboard or open metrics for that service.

savedVis.description: One hexagon per ECS service, ordered worst-first with CPU- or memory-saturated services first. Toggle Color by between Status, CPU utilization, or Memory utilization; metric views support Min, Max, Avg, and Last over the dashboard time range. Click any hexagon to filter the dashboard or open metrics for that service.
savedVis.title: ECS services — hexagon map

embeddableConfig.title: ECS services — hexagon map (click an hex for actions)

attributes.description: Number of idle ECS services — services whose latest CPU utilization is under 5%, within a 5-minute freshness window. May indicate over-provisioned or unused services.

| STATS cpu_cur = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",

embeddableConfig.description: Number of ECS services with no recent CPU or memory data — neither metric reported a value within the 5-minute freshness window.

embeddableConfig.description: Total number of ECS services — distinct services (cluster + service) that reported CPU utilization within the selected time range.

embeddableConfig.description: Number of ECS services in a normal state — those whose latest CPU or memory utilization is at or above 5% but below the hot threshold (not saturated, hot, or idle).

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-*
| WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != ""
  AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != ""
| STATS
    c_cpu_avg = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))) / 100.0,
    c_cpu_max = MAX(MAX_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Maximum",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))) / 100.0,
    c_mem_avg = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL))) / 100.0,
    c_mem_max = MAX(MAX_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Maximum",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL))) / 100.0,
    c_new_conn = SUM(SUM_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Sum",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/NewConnectionCount`, NULL))),
    c_active_conn = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ActiveConnectionCount`, NULL))),
    c_processed_bytes = SUM(SUM_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Sum",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ProcessedBytes`, NULL))),
    state_cpu = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))),
    state_mem = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL)))
  BY ServiceName, ClusterName, c_account = cloud.account.id, c_region = cloud.region
| EVAL
    c_state_sort = CASE(
      state_cpu IS NULL AND state_mem IS NULL, 4,
      state_cpu > 80 OR state_mem > 80, 1,
      state_cpu > 50 OR state_mem > 50, 2,
      3),
    c_state = CASE(
      state_cpu IS NULL AND state_mem IS NULL, "Unknown",
      state_cpu > 80 OR state_mem > 80, "saturated",
      state_cpu > 50 OR state_mem > 50, "hot",
      "healthy")
| SORT c_state_sort ASC

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-*
| WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != ""
  AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != ""
| STATS
    c_cpu_avg = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))) / 100.0,
    c_cpu_max = MAX(MAX_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Maximum",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))) / 100.0,
    c_mem_avg = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL))) / 100.0,
    c_mem_max = MAX(MAX_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Maximum",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL))) / 100.0,
    c_new_conn = SUM(SUM_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Sum",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/NewConnectionCount`, NULL))),
    c_active_conn = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ActiveConnectionCount`, NULL))),
    c_processed_bytes = SUM(SUM_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Sum",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ProcessedBytes`, NULL))),
    state_cpu = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))),
    state_mem = AVG(AVG_OVER_TIME(CASE(stat == "Average",
      `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL)))
  BY ServiceName, ClusterName, c_account = cloud.account.id, c_region = cloud.region
| EVAL
    c_state_sort = CASE(
      state_cpu IS NULL AND state_mem IS NULL, 4,
      state_cpu > 80 OR state_mem > 80, 1,
      state_cpu > 50 OR state_mem > 50, 2,
      3),
    c_state = CASE(
      state_cpu IS NULL AND state_mem IS NULL, "Unknown",
      state_cpu > 80 OR state_mem > 80, "saturated",
      state_cpu > 50 OR state_mem > 50, "hot",
      "healthy")
| SORT c_state_sort ASC

embeddableConfig.description: All ECS services, ranked worst-first by state. Click a service to drill into its metrics. State reflects the latest CPU/memory utilization within a 5-minute freshness window. Avg and Max CPU/memory are the average and peak utilization (%) over the selected time range; connection and processed-bytes columns are summed or averaged over the range.

drilldowns.label: View raw ECS metric stream in Discover

embeddableConfig.title: ECS services

attributes.description: Total new Service Connect connections across all ECS services, summed over the selected time range. Grows as the range widens.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/NewConnectionCount` IS NOT NULL AND stat == "Sum" | STATS new_conn = SUM(sum_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/NewConnectionCount`)) | KEEP new_conn

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/NewConnectionCount` IS NOT NULL AND stat == "Sum" | STATS new_conn = SUM(sum_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/NewConnectionCount`)) | KEEP new_conn

embeddableConfig.description: Total new Service Connect connections across all ECS services, summed over the selected time range. Grows as the range widens.

attributes.description: Average concurrent Service Connect connections across ECS services, averaged over the selected time range.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ActiveConnectionCount` IS NOT NULL AND stat == "Average" | STATS active_conn_avg = AVG(avg_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ActiveConnectionCount`)) | KEEP active_conn_avg

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ActiveConnectionCount` IS NOT NULL AND stat == "Average" | STATS active_conn_avg = AVG(avg_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ActiveConnectionCount`)) | KEEP active_conn_avg

embeddableConfig.description: Average concurrent Service Connect connections across ECS services, averaged over the selected time range.

attributes.description: Service Connect bytes processed per service over time — ProcessedBytes summed per time bucket (bytes). Limited to the top 10 services by processed bytes per bucket.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ProcessedBytes` IS NOT NULL AND stat == "Sum" | STATS col_bytes = SUM(sum_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ProcessedBytes`)) BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region | SORT timestamp ASC, col_bytes DESC, ServiceName ASC | LIMIT 10 BY timestamp

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ProcessedBytes` IS NOT NULL AND stat == "Sum" | STATS col_bytes = SUM(sum_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ProcessedBytes`)) BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region | SORT timestamp ASC, col_bytes DESC, ServiceName ASC | LIMIT 10 BY timestamp

attributes.title: Top 10 ECS services by bytes processed

embeddableConfig.description: Service Connect bytes processed per service over time — ProcessedBytes summed per time bucket (bytes). Limited to the top 10 services by processed bytes per bucket.
embeddableConfig.title: Top 10 ECS services by bytes processed

attributes.description: Top 10 ECS services by peak CPU utilization (%) over the selected time range (maximum per service). Reference line at 80% marks the high-CPU threshold; bars crossing it indicate services under sustained CPU pressure.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" AND stat == "Maximum" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization` IS NOT NULL | STATS peak_cpu = MAX(max_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`)) / 100.0 BY ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region | SORT peak_cpu DESC | LIMIT 10

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" AND stat == "Maximum" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization` IS NOT NULL | STATS peak_cpu = MAX(max_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`)) / 100.0 BY ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region | SORT peak_cpu DESC | LIMIT 10

attributes.title: Top 10 ECS services by CPU utilization

embeddableConfig.description: Top 10 ECS services by peak CPU utilization (%) over the selected time range (maximum per service). Reference line at 80% marks the high-CPU threshold; bars crossing it indicate services under sustained CPU pressure.
embeddableConfig.title: Top 10 ECS services by CPU utilization

attributes.description: Top 10 ECS services by peak memory utilization (%) over the selected time range. Bars approaching 100% risk out-of-memory task kills.

query.esql:
SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" AND stat == "Maximum" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization` IS NOT NULL | STATS peak_mem = MAX(max_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`)) / 100.0 BY ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region | SORT peak_mem DESC | LIMIT 10

SET unmapped_fields = "nullify";
TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" AND stat == "Maximum" AND `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization` IS NOT NULL | STATS peak_mem = MAX(max_over_time(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`)) / 100.0 BY ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region | SORT peak_mem DESC | LIMIT 10
